terms.definition: A radiocommunication service between earth stations at specified fixed points (ITU Radio Regulations No. 1.21). Satellite communications service in which Earth stations at fixed points communicate via a satellite.
terms.short_definition: Satellite service between fixed earth stations (ITU RR No. 1.21).

terms.definition: Satellite radiocommunication service in which mobile earth stations communicate via satellite (ITU Radio Regulations).
terms.short_definition: Satellite service for mobile earth stations.

terms.definition: Agency in the US Department of Commerce that manages spectrum used by federal government agencies and advises the President on telecommunications policy.
terms.short_definition: US federal spectrum manager (Dept. of Commerce).

terms.definition: Independent US agency that regulates interstate communications and licenses non-federal spectrum use.
terms.short_definition: US spectrum regulator for non-federal use.

terms.definition: US executive department responsible for coordinating and supervising the federal armed forces; major incumbent operator of federal spectrum allocations including portions of the 7 GHz band.
terms.short_definition: Incumbent operator of federal spectrum in many bands.

terms.definition: Working group of federal agency representatives tasked with technical analysis for a specific line of effort within an NTIA-led spectrum study.
terms.short_definition: Agency working group doing technical analysis (NTIA).

terms.definition: Parent body coordinating multiple TIGs in a spectrum study (e.g. the 7 GHz study).
terms.short_definition: Parent body coordinating multiple TIGs.

terms.definition: Subcommittee of subject-matter experts that performs detailed technical work in support of an interagency or international spectrum coordination process.
terms.short_definition: Subcommittee of technical SMEs supporting coordination.

terms.definition: 3GPP working group 4 on Radio Access Network performance, responsible for RF requirements and coexistence studies. Frequent counterpart in spectrum coordination between mobile cellular standards and incumbent services.
terms.short_definition: 3GPP working group on RF coexistence and RAN performance.

terms.definition: United Nations agency that coordinates global spectrum use through the Radio Regulations and study groups.
terms.short_definition: UN agency coordinating global spectrum use.

terms.definition: Proposed approach for spectrum transition documented with feasibility and impact analysis. Comparable to an option in a decision memo.
terms.short_definition: Proposed spectrum-transition approach with analysis.

terms.definition: Governing document for an NTIA spectrum study defining scope, methodology, deliverables, and schedule.
terms.short_definition: Governing document for an NTIA spectrum study.

terms.definition: Geographic area in which new spectrum entrants must not cause harmful interference to FSS Earth stations. Distance based on link budget and interference threshold.
terms.short_definition: Geographic area protecting FSS earth stations from new entrants.

terms.definition: Interference threshold that must not be exceeded to ensure acceptable performance of an incumbent service. In the 7 GHz downlink study: -10.5 dB I/N at 80th percentile and -6 dB at 0.03 percent.
terms.short_definition: Interference threshold protecting an incumbent service.

terms.definition: Protection standard requiring two separate exceedance thresholds: -10.5 dB not exceeded more than 20 percent of time AND -6 dB not exceeded more than 0.03 percent of time.
terms.short_definition: ITU two-threshold interference protection rule.

terms.definition: Total power radiated by a transmitting antenna in a given direction, computed from transmitter power and antenna gain.
terms.short_definition: Total transmitter+antenna radiated power in a direction.

terms.definition: Ratio of received interference power to receiver noise power. Standard ITU metric used to evaluate the impact of one service on another.
terms.short_definition: Ratio of interference to receiver noise (ITU metric).

terms.definition: Statistical function giving the probability that a random variable is less than or equal to a value. Used in ITU protection criteria to define percentile thresholds.
terms.short_definition: Statistical CDF (used for ITU percentile thresholds).

terms.definition: Propagation model that predicts long-term median attenuation over irregular terrain. Widely used in US interference analyses.
terms.short_definition: Long-term terrain propagation model used in interference analysis.

terms.definition: Combined interference from all sources affecting a receiver, as opposed to interference from a single transmitter.
terms.short_definition: Combined interference from all sources at a receiver.

terms.definition: Unused or restricted frequency separation between two adjacent frequency allocations or channels to limit interference.
terms.short_definition: Frequency separation between adjacent spectrum users.

terms.definition: Highest protection status in a spectrum allocation; primary services may not be required to accept interference from secondary services.
terms.short_definition: Highest-protection allocation status.

terms.definition: Lower protection status; secondary services must accept interference from primary services and may not cause harmful interference to primary services.
terms.short_definition: Lower-protection allocation status; must yield to primary.

terms.definition: Agency plan governing how operations move out of a spectrum band being reallocated to a new use.
terms.short_definition: Plan for moving an agency out of a reallocated band.

terms.definition: Movement of a spectrum user from one frequency band to another, typically in response to a reallocation decision.
terms.short_definition: Movement of a user from one frequency band to another.
